display.purpose: Landing hero and wordmark. Zilla Slab 700 italic, once per view.

title.purpose: Card headings, show/episode H1s (italic), list and feature names.

body.purpose: All running text. Cap measure at 65-75ch.

label.purpose: Uppercase section eyebrows with an amber tick, small stat labels.

mono.purpose: Printed-on-the-tape metadata: slates, dates, counts, faux URLs.

shadows.name: overlay-lift
shadows.purpose: Modal dialogs only, the one surface that truly floats.

shadows.name: hero-frame
shadows.purpose: Framed product screenshots on the landing page.

shadows.name: on-air-glow
shadows.purpose: The only colored shadow, and only on the live airing dot.

motion.name: btn-press
motion.purpose: Button hover brighten and active scale(0.98).

motion.name: check-flip
motion.purpose: Episode check toggling on, with a scale(0.9) active dip.

motion.name: progress-fill
motion.purpose: Progress bar filling as episodes are marked.

motion.name: reduced-motion
motion.purpose: prefers-reduced-motion collapses all transitions and animations.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The one loud control on a screen: solid tungsten amber, used for the single most important action.
components.css: .ds-btn { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 7px; font: 600 14px "Figtree", sans-serif; border-radius: 8px; padding: 8px 16px; cursor: pointer; border: 1px solid transparent; transition: filter 0.15s, transform 0.1s; } .ds-btn-primary { background: var(--amber, #ffae2e); color: var(--amber-ink, #1a1205); border-color: var(--amber, #ffae2e); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { filter: brightness(1.08); } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: scale(0.98); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--amber, #ffae2e); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Secondary action: transparent with a hairline border. Hover lifts the border, no brightness shift.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 7px; font: 600 14px "Figtree", sans-serif; border-radius: 8px; padding: 8px 16px; cursor: pointer; background: transparent; color: var(--text, #ede9e0); border: 1px solid var(--line, #2a3344); transition: border-color 0.15s; } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { border-color: var(--muted, #8e97a8); } .ds-btn-ghost:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--amber, #ffae2e); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Slate Chip
components.description: The signature production slate: a mono episode code, amber on amber-wash. The product's most-repeated brand atom.
components.css: .ds-slate { font: 600 12px "Spline Sans Mono", monospace; letter-spacing: 0.04em; color: var(--amber, #ffae2e); background: rgba(255,174,46,0.14); border-radius: 4px; padding: 1.5px 6px; white-space: nowrap; }

components.name: Search Field
components.description: Pill-shaped tuner readout with an inline magnifier. The header/search field idiom.
components.css: .ds-search { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; background: var(--surface, #171c26); border: 1px solid var(--line, #2a3344); border-radius: 999px; padding: 5px 14px; color: var(--muted, #8e97a8); } .ds-search input { background: none; border: none; color: var(--text, #ede9e0); font: 400 15px "Figtree", sans-serif; width: 190px; outline: none; } .ds-search:focus-within { outline: 2px solid var(--amber, #ffae2e); outline-offset: 1px; }

components.name: Progress Bar
components.description: Watch progress: a thin amber fill on a surface-riser track, animating its width as episodes are marked.
components.css: .ds-progress { height: 4px; width: 180px; background: var(--surface-2, #202836); border-radius: 2px; overflow: hidden; } .ds-progress-fill { height: 100%; background: var(--amber, #ffae2e); border-radius: 2px; transition: width 0.3s; }

components.name: SMPTE Bars
components.description: Signature section divider and brand punctuation: a seven-band broadcast color-bar strip.
components.css: .ds-smpte { height: 6px; width: 100%; border-radius: 3px; opacity: 0.85; background: linear-gradient(90deg, #b8b8ac 0 14.3%, #b8b855 0 28.6%, #55b8ac 0 42.9%, #55b855 0 57.2%, #b855ac 0 71.5%, #b85555 0 85.8%, #5555b8 0 100%); }

components.name: ON AIR Dot
components.description: Airing-now status light: a glowing red dot for live, a hollow hairline ring for a future air date. Color plus shape, never color alone.
components.css: .ds-onair { display: inline-block; width: 8px; height: 8px; border-radius: 50%; background: var(--red, #ff4d3d); box-shadow: 0 0 6px rgba(255,77,61,0.8); } .ds-onair-future { background: none; box-shadow: none; border: 1.5px solid var(--line, #2a3344); }

components.name: Feature Card
components.description: Flat content card: surface fill, hairline border, an amber-wash icon tile. No shadow, separation is tonal.
components.html:
Watch Next queue

Every show you follow, lined up to its exact next episode.
components.css: .ds-card { background: var(--surface, #171c26); border: 1px solid var(--line, #2a3344); border-radius: 10px; padding: 18px; max-width: 260px; } .ds-card-icon { display: inline-grid; place-items: center; width: 38px; height: 38px; border-radius: 10px; background: rgba(255,174,46,0.14); color: var(--amber, #ffae2e); margin-bottom: 12px; } .ds-card-title { font: 700 16px "Zilla Slab", serif; letter-spacing: -0.01em; color: var(--text, #ede9e0); margin: 0 0 6px; } .ds-card-body { font: 400 13.5px "Figtree", sans-serif; line-height: 1.55; color: var(--muted, #8e97a8); margin: 0; }

narrative.overview: Show Us TV looks like the room where the tape lived. A dim slate wall, one warm tungsten dial you keep reaching for, a cyan readout ticking off the schedule, and a red light that means something is on the air right now. The whole product runs on the vocabulary of analog broadcast, SMPTE color bars, production slates, the ON AIR bulb, a TV bug in the wordmark, but the nostalgia is warm and worn, not neon. Density is calm and legible: the working surfaces stay quiet and fast; the personality lives in the chrome, the empty states, the wordmark, and the copy, never in the path of the one tap that matters.
keyCharacteristics: Deep slate room, dark by conviction, not by trend.
One warm accent (tungsten amber) that only ever means action or progress.
A three-light status language borrowed from a broadcast rack: red ON AIR, amber acting, green done.
Broadcast props that carry real meaning: slates for episode codes, SMPTE bars for section breaks.
Zilla Slab italic for identity, Spline Sans Mono for the printed-on-the-tape details.

rules.name: The Three-Light Rule
rules.body: Status is spoken in exactly three colored lights, red on air, amber acting, green done, plus cyan for schedule/info. A surface never invents a fifth status color; it reuses one of these four with an icon or label beside it.

rules.name: The One Bulb Rule
rules.body: Amber is the only warm light in the room and it means action or progress, nothing else. It is never a decorative accent, a heading color, or a background field. Its scarcity is what makes a primary button obvious.

rules.name: The Printed-On-The-Tape Rule
rules.body: Anything that would be physically stamped on a cassette or a shot list, episode codes, air dates, counts, runtimes, is set in Spline Sans Mono. Prose is never mono; metadata is never prose-set.

rules.name: The Italic-Is-The-Brand Rule
rules.body: The forward-leaning italic is reserved for the wordmark, the landing hero, and title-level show names. It is an identity signal, not emphasis; body copy never leans.

rules.name: The Flat-Room Rule
rules.body: Cards, tiles, chips, and panels are flat, distinguished by a lighter slate fill and a 1px line border. Shadows are for things that truly float (dialogs, the hero photo) and for the ON AIR glow. Nothing else.

dos: Do keep amber to one job. A primary button, active state, or progress fill, never a heading color or decorative fill (the One Bulb Rule).

Do speak status in the three lights plus cyan: red ON AIR, amber acting, green done, cyan schedule/info, and always pair the color with an icon, label, or code so color is never the only signal (WCAG 2.2 AA).
Do build surfaces from the four slate steps (bg/surface/surface-2/line) and a 1px hairline. Depth is tonal, not shadowed (the Flat-Room Rule).
Do set every episode code, date, count, and runtime in Spline Sans Mono; reserve Zilla Slab italic for the wordmark, landing hero, and show titles.
Do use warm-white #ede9e0 for text and the slate neutrals for chrome. Tint neutrals toward blue-slate; never reach for pure #fff or #000.
Do give the ON AIR dot its soft red glow, the one colored shadow in the system, and grow tap targets to at least 40px on mobile.
donts: Don't make it a streaming-service clone: no Netflix/Max glossy-dark hero carousels or browse-and-stream autoplay. This is a tracker, not a storefront.
Don't let it drift into corporate SaaS sameness: no flat-gray Linear/Notion dashboard template, no endless identical icon+heading+text card grids.
Don't ship generic Material/Bootstrap admin defaults: no purple primary buttons, no stock elevation, no off-the-shelf component library look.
Don't go neon gamer/cinema: no glow-on-black, no aggressive gradients, no cyberpunk. The retro is warm analog, not synthwave.
Don't add drop shadows to separate flat surfaces, if a card doesn't read, fix the slate step, not the shadow.
Don't use background-clip: text gradient text, decorative glassmorphism, or a border-left/border-right colored stripe wider than 1px as an accent.
Don't print prose in mono or lean body text into italic, those are reserved signals.
